name: Blob Storage Operations from the CLI
description: Create storage accounts, manage blob containers, upload and download files, and set access tiers using the Azure CLI.

steps.title: Create a storage account
steps.command: az storage account create --name mystorageacct --resource-group myapp-rg --location eastus --sku Standard_LRS
steps.description: Creates a general-purpose v2 storage account with locally redundant storage.
steps.tip: Storage account names must be globally unique, 3-24 characters, and lowercase alphanumeric only.

steps.title: Create a blob container
steps.command: az storage container create --name uploads --account-name mystorageacct --auth-mode login
steps.description: Creates a private blob container. The --auth-mode login uses your Azure AD credentials.

steps.title: Upload a file
steps.command: az storage blob upload --account-name mystorageacct --container-name uploads --file ./report.pdf --name reports/2026/report.pdf --auth-mode login
steps.description: Uploads a local file to a specific blob path in the container.

steps.title: List blobs in a container
steps.command: az storage blob list --account-name mystorageacct --container-name uploads --auth-mode login --output table
steps.description: Shows all blobs in the container with size and last modified date.

steps.title: Download a blob
steps.command: az storage blob download --account-name mystorageacct --container-name uploads --name reports/2026/report.pdf --file ./downloaded-report.pdf --auth-mode login
steps.description: Downloads a blob to a local file path.

fullScript:
# Azure Blob Storage operations
az storage account create --name mystorageacct --resource-group myapp-rg --location eastus --sku Standard_LRS
az storage container create --name uploads --account-name mystorageacct --auth-mode login
az storage blob upload --account-name mystorageacct --container-name uploads --file ./report.pdf --name reports/2026/report.pdf --auth-mode login
az storage blob list --account-name mystorageacct --container-name uploads --auth-mode login --output table
az storage blob download --account-name mystorageacct --container-name uploads --name reports/2026/report.pdf --file ./downloaded-report.pdf --auth-mode login

faq.question: What is the difference between Hot, Cool, and Archive tiers?
faq.answer: Hot is for frequently accessed data (lowest access cost, higher storage cost). Cool is for data accessed less than once a month. Archive is for rarely accessed data with the lowest storage cost but hours-long retrieval times.

faq.question: How do I generate a SAS token for temporary access?
faq.answer: Use 'az storage blob generate-sas --account-name mystorageacct --container-name uploads --name myfile --permissions r --expiry 2026-03-12T00:00Z'. This creates a time-limited read-only URL.
